mechanism_narrative: Persistent growth in real retail sales signals robust consumer demand, which translates into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Retail sales data available through Oct 2005
failure_condition: Walk-forward 20-day forward returns following month-over-month RSAFS growth are not positive in more than 55% of instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, sustained elevated levels restrict credit availability and raise discount rates, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Federal funds rate at 3.78% as of Oct 2005
failure_condition: Walk-forward 60-day forward returns during periods with FEDFUNDS > 4.5% are negative in fewer than 55% of triggered instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous rising unemployment and declining nonfarm payrolls signal a contraction in labor income that reduces consumer spending and corporate earnings expectations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising UNRATE and falling PAYEMS fails to produce negative 20-day returns in more than 60% of coincident months during walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, accelerating industrial production growth indicates supply-side constraints that amplify input cost pressures and squeeze corporate margins, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 107.055 in Sep 2005 amid rising rates
failure_condition: During monetary tightening cycles, positive INDPRO momentum does not coincide with negative 20-day SPX returns in at least 55% of months during walk-forward evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in housing starts reflect strengthening residential investment and construction activity, which drives demand for materials, labor, and credit, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in HOUST are followed by negative 5-day SPX returns in more than 45% of observations over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year CPI inflation exceeds 3.5%, equity markets reprice forward earnings multiples downward due to expectations of persistent monetary tightening, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPI at 198.5 in Sep 2005 reflecting elevated inflation
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months when CPIAUCSL YoY exceeds 3.5% during walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of elevated federal funds rates and declining retail sales creates a credit-demand contraction where tighter monetary policy simultaneously raises financing costs and weakens consumption, producing strongly negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The bivariate signal of elevated FEDFUNDS and declining RSAFS does not produce negative 20-day returns in more than 60% of coincident observations during walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month rise in the unemployment rate of at least 0.2 percentage points signals rapid labor market deterioration that reduces near-term consumption and earnings visibility, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: UNRATE increases of at least 0.2 percentage points are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 50% of instances during walk-forward testing.
